title: 10 best herbal teas for runny nose

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with runny nose because its active compound, echinacea, has natural antihistamine properties that effectively combat inflammation in the nasal passages. As a result, it reduces excess mucus production and congestion, alleviating the discomfort of a runny nose. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe the nasal tissues, further reducing nasal drip and congestion, allowing for easier breathing and a sense of relief from colds and allergies.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Kansas Coneflower tea's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and runny nose symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cichoric acid and echinacin, which work to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with a runny nose.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas Coneflower tea's immunomodulatory properties help stimulate the immune system, enhancing its natural response to infections that can cause a runny nose, such as common colds and allergies.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are used to soothe nasal passages and reduce excess mucus production.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, bark is employed to calm inflammation and congestion in the nasal cavity.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers per cup of boiling water and measure accurately.
Heat two cups of fresh filtered water in a medium saucepan to a rolling boil for five minutes.
Add the measured dried echinacea flowers to the boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool slightly and drink immediately, re-steeping as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with runny nose because of its natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as cinnamic acid and flavonoids, work together to reduce nasal congestion and swelling, thereby alleviating sinus pressure and relieving a runny nose. Additionally, plantain's mucilages help soothe and calm irritated nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and a clearer sense of smell.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like allantoin, which help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating a runny nose.
Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Plantain's antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause a runny nose, such as the common cold or sinusitis.
Mucilages and demulcents: The mucilages and demulcents in Plantain tea create a soothing, protective barrier on the nasal membranes, helping to calm irritation and reduce excess mucus production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Barks: Plantain barks contain tannins, which help dry up excess mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves for brewing at dawn or dusk when potency is highest.
Rinse the collected plantago major leaves thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine one tablespoon of dried plantago major leaves or four teaspoons of fresh plantago major leaves with one cup boiling water for ten minutes steeping time.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids and discard leaves.
Drink the herbal tea immediately while still warm three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers teas helps with runny nose because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The tea's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and congestion in the sinuses. Additionally, cleavers' expectorant properties help loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it easier to breathe and alleviating sinus pressure. By addressing these underlying causes of runny nose, cleavers teas provides relief from annoying symptoms and promotes a sense of clarity and comfort.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Cleaver tea's astringent properties help to reduce excessive mucus production and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in cleaver tea may help to alleviate symptoms of rhinitis (inflammation of the mucous membranes) and reduce nasal discharge.
Expectorant properties: Cleaver tea's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the respiratory tract, providing temporary relief from a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing excess mucus production.
Stems: Contain astringent properties that help dry up excess mucus, relieving congestion and runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried galium aparine leaves and flowers.
Heat one cup of water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches boiling point.
Steep the herb mixture in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Allow the tea to cool down for 10-15 minutes before drinking it while warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with runny nose because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce congestion and swelling in the nasal passages. The tea's antihistamine properties also work to block the release of histamines, which can contribute to excess mucus production. Additionally, dandelion's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out stubborn mucus, providing quick relief from a runny nose. Drinking dandelion teas regularly may even help to reduce the frequency and severity of sinus pressure and congestion.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Dandelion tea's antihistamine and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce nasal congestion, relieve sinus pressure, and alleviate allergies-related symptoms like a runny nose.
Expectorant Properties: The tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing the sensation of a runny nose.

Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion tea's antioxidants help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to congestion, sinus pressure, and a runny nose by reducing free radicals that exacerbate these symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to combat inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried dandelion flowers taraxacum officinale and store them airtight.
Measure one teaspoon of dried dandelion flowers per cup of boiling water for every serving.
Combine measured dandelion flowers with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors to meld and the active compounds to release.
Strain the tea into a cup discard the solids and drink immediately while warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with runny nose because of its natural antihistamine properties. The herb contains compounds like citral and citronellal, which have been shown to reduce histamine production in the body, thereby alleviating congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages, further reducing the frequency and severity of runny nose episodes. By drinking lemon verbena tea regularly, individuals can experience a reduction in symptoms associated with allergies and colds, promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antihistaminic properties: Lemon verbena tea helps to reduce histamine levels, which can alleviate nasal congestion and relieve a runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, leading to a decrease in mucus production and runny nose symptoms.
Decongestant properties: Lemon verbena tea contains volatile oils that can help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and providing relief from a stuffy or runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing nasal congestion and alleviating sinus pressure.
Leaves: The leaves of lemon verbena contain flavonoids that help to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Measure out two cups of filtered water in a medium-sized saucepan and bring it to a rolling boil on high heat.
Remove the boiling water from heat and let it steep with the dried verbena officinalis mixture for ten minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large teapot or individual cups, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for five minutes before serving hot, with honey or lemon to taste, and enjoy within two hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with runny nose because it contains menthol, a natural decongestant that effectively relieves nasal congestion and sinus pressure. The menthol in peppermint tea helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, which in turn reduces the production of excess mucus and alleviates a runny nose. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help to calm and soothe the nasal passages, providing instant relief from discomfort and congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
Expectorant: The menthol content in peppermint tea acts as an expectorant, loosening and thinning mucus, making it easier to expel, thus reducing the runniness of the nose.
Decongestant: Peppermint's natural decongestant properties help reduce blood flow to the nasal tissues, decreasing swelling and reducing mucus production, which in turn alleviates a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other compounds that help to relieve congestion and soothe the nasal passages.
Leaves: Contains menthone, which acts as a natural decongestant and helps to reduce mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase dried peppermint leaves in bulk quantities.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Pour the measured hot water over the peppermint leaves in a heat-resistant cup or teapot and let steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer into another cup or container.
Serve the cooled peppermint tea warm or at room temperature as needed, ideally twice daily to help alleviate runny nose symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel. Drinking a warm cup of thyme tea can also help to ease nasal irritation and discomfort caused by colds, flu, or allergies, promoting a sense of relief and clarity in the nasal passages.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to your runny nose.
Expectorant properties: The saponins in thyme tea help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure, further alleviating a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain antibacterial properties that help reduce congestion and soothe a runny nose.
Flowers: Thyme flowers have anti-inflammatory compounds that alleviate nasal swelling and ease breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh thyme leaves or dried thyme powder from reputable sources weighing approximately one teaspoon per cup.
Bring filtered water to a gentle boil and reduce heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes to prepare the tea base.
Add the harvested thyme leaves or powdered thyme to the hot water and steep for an additional 5-10 minutes allowing the flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Consume the herbal thyme tea within 20-30 minutes of preparation when the steam rises to effectively alleviate runny nose symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with runny nose because of its natural antihistamine properties, which help to reduce histamine production in the body. The soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds in sage also ease congestion and calm the mucous membranes, allowing for a decrease in mucus production and a reduction in nasal discharge. Additionally, sage's decongestant properties help to shrink swollen blood vessels in the nose, further alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage tea contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, which can alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols present in sage tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal tissues, making it easier to breathe and reducing mucus production.
Expectorant properties: Sage tea has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Flowers: Sage flowers contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and promoting drainage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Measure one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine measured leaves and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil then reduce heat to simmer.
Steep mixture for 5-7 minutes or until leaves lose their bright green color and aroma is released.
Strain tea into a cup and discard solids drink immediately or store in refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with runny nose because it contains natural decongestants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure. The warm steam from the tea can also help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it easier to breathe through the nose. Additionally, many herbal teas, such as peppermint and eucalyptus, have natural expectorant properties that help to thin and expel mucus, providing relief from a runny nose and sinus congestion.
remedy_properties: Decongestant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal teas like peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger contain decongestant and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce nasal congestion and swelling, relieving a runny nose.

Antihistamine properties: Teas made from herbs like chamomile, lemon balm, and passionflower have antihistamine properties that can help reduce the body's histamine response, which can contribute to a runny nose and other allergy symptoms.

Expectorant and mucolytic properties: Teas like thyme, eucalyptus, and slippery elm contain expectorant and mucolytic compounds that help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and discomfort associated with a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contains bioactive compounds that help to reduce nasal congestion and relieve sinus pressure.
Rhyzomes: Has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing the severity of runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves in two cups of water for five minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or cup immediately.
Add one half teaspoon of honey and stir until dissolved slowly.
Let the tea steep for an additional three to five minutes before serving warm.
Drink the entire concoction twice daily as needed for optimal nasal relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The tea's flavonoids also work to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion. Additionally, licorice root contains compounds that have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, calming any irritation or inflammation and promoting healthy drainage. As a result, herbal licorice teas can provide relief from a runny nose caused by allergies, colds, or sinus infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and runny nose.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of licorice tea help loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to expel from the body and reducing congestion and runny nose.
Antiviral and antimicrobial activities: Licorice tea has antiviral and antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections caused by viruses and bacteria, which can contribute to a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing congestion and relieving runny nose.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate sinus pressure associated with a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried licorice roots or use pre-packaged organic licorice root powder for the best flavor and effectiveness.
Boil one teaspoon of dried licorice root or two teaspoons of licorice root powder in four cups of water for 5-7 minutes.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let it steep for an additional 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or mug to remove any remaining root particles and solids.
Let the tea cool slightly before drinking, then enjoy as needed up to three times daily for optimal relief from runny nose symptoms.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for runny nose are a natural remedy that can provide relief from congestion, sinus pressure, and constant sniffles. These teas work by soothing and calming the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and mucus production. Examples of herbal teas that help with runny nose include peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger tea. Drinking these teas can improve daily life by providing instant relief from uncomfortable symptoms, allowing for a clearer breath, and enhancing overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat runny nose with herbal teas is to opt for soothing and decongestant options. Peppermint tea helps clear nasal passages and ease congestion, while eucalyptus tea reduces inflammation and loosens mucus. Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Drink 2-3 cups of herbal tea per day, warm or at room temperature, to experience relief from a runny nose.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal teas are congestion, sinusitis, and allergies. Herbal teas such as peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger have natural decongestant properties that help relieve nasal passages and ease breathing. Other herbs like nettle, chamomile, and lemon balm also provide relief from allergic reactions and sinus pressure, helping to soothe the throat and calm irritated mucous membranes.
